full_response:
We want the directional derivative of an arbitrary vector field \(V^a\) along an arbitrary vector field \(W^a\).  By definition in a (pseudo-)Riemannian manifold with Levi-Civita connection, this is

  \((W\!\cdot\!\nabla)V\)^a \;=\; \(W^b\nabla_b V^a\)\,.

Writing out the covariant derivative \(\nabla_b V^a = \partial_b V^a + \Gamma^a_{\,bc}V^c\), we get

  \((W\!\cdot\!\nabla)V\)^a 
  = \(W^b\bigl(\partial_b V^a + \Gamma^a_{\,bc}V^c\bigr)\)\,.

This is the general expression for the directional derivative of \(V^a\) in the direction \(W^a\).

\[
\boxed{
(W\!\cdot\!\nabla)V)^a \;=\; W^b\,\nabla_b V^a
\;=\;
W^b\bigl(\partial_b V^a + \Gamma^a_{\,bc}\,V^c\bigr)
}
\]

Confidence: 100%
